title: Avocado Configuration
description: Configuration schema for Avocado CLI avocado.yaml files. Defines project settings, build configurations, packages, and provisioning profiles for Avocado OS projects.

default_target.title: Default target
default_target.description: The default target platform for the project. This target is used when building the project if no target is specified.

supported_targets.title: Supported targets

oneOf.description: List of specific supported target architectures.

supported_targets.description: Defines which targets are supported by this project. Use '*' to support all targets, or provide an array of specific target names to limit support.

src_dir.title: Source directory
src_dir.description: Path to the source directory relative to the configuration file. If not specified, defaults to the directory containing the configuration file. This is where your project source code is located.

distro.title: Distribution configuration
distro.description: Distribution-specific configuration for the Avocado OS project.

runtimes.title: Runtimes
runtimes.description: Runtime configurations for different deployment scenarios. Each runtime can specify extensions, packages, and target-specific settings.

provision_profiles.title: Provisioning profiles
provision_profiles.description: Provisioning profiles for deploying Avocado OS images to devices. Each profile can define different settings for development, production, or custom deployment scenarios.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.description: Extension configurations for both local extensions (defined in this file) and remote extensions (fetched from package repositories, git, or local paths).

cli_requirement.title: CLI version requirement
cli_requirement.description: Minimum Avocado CLI version required to use this configuration. If the running CLI version does not satisfy this requirement, an error is raised with an upgrade prompt. Uses semver requirement syntax.

source_date_epoch.title: Source date epoch
source_date_epoch.description: Fixed Unix timestamp used for reproducible extension image builds. When set, this value is passed to mksquashfs and mkfs.erofs as the source timestamp, ensuring identical byte output for the same inputs regardless of build time. Defaults to 0 when not specified.

signing_keys.title: Signing keys
signing_keys.description: Mapping of friendly names to signing key IDs. Acts as a local bridge between the config and the global signing keys registry.

version.title: Version
version.description: A version string. Use '*' for any version, or a specific semantic version.

versionRequirement.title: Version requirement
versionRequirement.description: Semantic version requirement string. Supports exact versions (e.g., '2.1.3'), minimum versions (e.g., '>=1.0.0'), ranges, and other semver patterns.

target.title: Target
target.description: An identifier for the platform for Avocado OS builds and deployments.

distroConfig.title: Distribution configuration
distroConfig.description: Distribution-specific settings including feed year, stability channel, and repository configuration.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Distribution stability channel (e.g., 'edge', 'stable'). Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_DISTRO_CHANNEL environment variable.

release.title: Release
release.description: Distribution feed year (e.g., '2024' or 2024). Accepts both strings and integers. Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_DISTRO_RELEASE environment variable. Previously named 'version' which is still accepted as an alias.

repo.title: Repository configuration
repo.description: Repository configuration for package downloads. Replaces the deprecated sdk.repo_url and sdk.repo_release fields.

distroRepoConfig.title: Distribution repository configuration
distroRepoConfig.description: Repository URL and release version configuration. When releasever is not set, it is derived from '{release}/{channel}' (e.g., '2024/edge').

url.title: Repository URL
url.description: URL of the package repository. Defaults to https://repo.avocadolinux.org. Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_REPO_URL environment variable (or legacy AVOCADO_SDK_REPO_URL).

releasever.title: Release version
releasever.description: Explicit releasever override for DNF --releasever. When not set, derived from distro.release and distro.channel as '{release}/{channel}'. Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_RELEASEVER environment variable (or legacy AVOCADO_SDK_REPO_RELEASE).

extensionSource.title: Extension source
extensionSource.description: Source configuration for fetching a remote extension. Supports package repositories, git repositories, and local filesystem paths.

oneOf.title: Package source
oneOf.description: Extension from the Avocado package repository.

package.description: Optional RPM package name (defaults to extension name if not specified).

include.description: Optional list of config sections to include from the remote extension. Supports dot-separated paths (e.g., 'provision_profiles.tegraflash') and wildcards (e.g., 'provision_profiles.*'). The extension's own section is always included.

ref.description: Git ref (branch, tag, or commit hash).

include.description: Optional list of config sections to include from the remote extension.

oneOf.title: Path source
oneOf.description: Extension from a local filesystem path.

path.description: Path to the extension directory (relative to config or absolute).

include.description: Optional list of config sections to include from the extension.

runtimeConfig.title: Runtime configuration
runtimeConfig.description: Configuration for a runtime deployment scenario.

target.description: Target platform for this runtime (overrides default_target).

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.description: List of extension names to include in this runtime. Extensions must be defined in the 'extensions' section.

stone_include_paths.description: Additional stone include paths for this runtime.

stone_manifest.description: Path to a custom stone manifest file.

var_files.title: Var files
var_files.description: Static files to copy from the source directory onto the var partition at build time. Each entry maps a source path (relative to src_dir) to a destination path on the var partition. Directories are copied recursively. The dest for a directory should end with /.

kernel.title: Kernel configuration
kernel.description: Kernel configuration for this runtime. Use 'package' to install a pre-built kernel RPM, or 'compile'+'install' to cross-compile and install a custom kernel.

package.description: Pre-built kernel RPM package name to install.

version.description: Version constraint for the kernel package.

compile.description: References an sdk.compile section to use for kernel compilation.

install.description: Path to install script relative to src_dir, run after kernel compilation with $AVOCADO_RUNTIME_BUILD_DIR set.

varFileMapping.title: Var file mapping
varFileMapping.description: A source-to-destination mapping for seeding static files onto the var partition at build time.

source.description: Source path relative to src_dir. Can be a file or directory. Directories are copied recursively.

dest.description: Destination path relative to the var partition root (e.g., 'lib/myapp/' maps to '/var/lib/myapp/' on the device). End with / for directories.

key.description: Name of the signing key to use (references a key from signing_keys section).

checksum_algorithm.description: Checksum algorithm to use for signing.

sdkConfig.description: SDK configuration for building Avocado OS projects. Supports target-specific overrides using target names as keys.

targetSdkConfig.description: SDK configuration for building Avocado OS projects.

image.title: Docker image
image.description: Docker image to use for the SDK build environment. This provides the toolchain and build tools necessary for compilation.

packages.title: Packages
packages.description: SDK-level packages required for building the project. These are installed in the build environment.

compile.title: Compile configurations
compile.description: Compile configurations for specific extensions or components. Each entry defines how to build a particular extension.

repo_url.title: Repository URL (deprecated)
repo_url.description: Deprecated: use distro.repo.url instead. URL of the package repository. Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_REPO_URL environment variable (or legacy AVOCADO_SDK_REPO_URL).

repo_release.title: Repository release (deprecated)
repo_release.description: Deprecated: use distro.repo.releasever instead. Explicit releasever for DNF --releasever. Can be overridden with the AVOCADO_RELEASEVER environment variable (or legacy AVOCADO_SDK_REPO_RELEASE).

container_args.title: Container arguments
container_args.description: Additional arguments to pass to the Docker container. Supports environment variable expansion using ${VAR_NAME} syntax.

disable_weak_dependencies.title: Disable weak dependencies
disable_weak_dependencies.description: Disable installation of weak dependencies when installing packages.

host_uid.title: Host UID
host_uid.description: Host UID for bindfs permission translation (overrides libc::getuid()).

host_gid.title: Host GID
host_gid.description: Host GID for bindfs permission translation (overrides libc::getgid()).

compileConfig.title: Compile configuration
compileConfig.description: Compilation configuration for a specific extension or component.

compile.title: Compile command
compile.description: Compile command or script to execute for building this component.

clean.title: Clean command
clean.description: Path to clean script relative to src_dir, executed during 'ext clean' command.

packages.title: Packages
packages.description: Build-time packages required for compiling this component.

package.title: Package configuration
package.description: RPM packaging metadata. When present, 'avocado sdk package <section>' can produce one or more architecture-specific RPMs from this compile section's output.

packageConfig.title: Package configuration
packageConfig.description: RPM packaging metadata for a compiled SDK section. Enables 'avocado sdk package' to produce an installable RPM.

name.description: RPM package name. Defaults to the compile section name if not specified.

version.description: Package version in semver format. Required.

install.description: Path to install script relative to src_dir. The script is called with $DESTDIR set to a staging directory; files staged there are packaged into the RPM. Required.

examples: A cross-compiled shared library for the target.

arch.description: Override the target architecture in the RPM spec. Defaults to the build target architecture.

url.description: URL for the package homepage or source repository.

requires.description: RPM Requires dependencies for the package.

files.description: Glob patterns for files to include in the main package (relative to $DESTDIR). Defaults to all files if not specified.

split.description: Optional sub-package splitting (Yocto-style). Each key is a sub-package name; each value is a splitPackageConfig.

splitPackageConfig.title: Split package configuration
splitPackageConfig.description: Configuration for a sub-package produced by splitting a compiled SDK section.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the sub-package.

files.description: Glob patterns for files to include in this sub-package (relative to $DESTDIR). Files claimed by a sub-package are excluded from the main package.

provisionProfileConfig.title: Provision profile configuration
provisionProfileConfig.description: Configuration for a provisioning profile used to deploy Avocado OS images to devices.

container_args.title: Container arguments
container_args.description: Additional arguments to pass to the provisioning container. Supports environment variable expansion using ${VAR_NAME} syntax.

state_file.title: State file
state_file.description: Path to state file relative to src_dir for persisting state between provision runs. Defaults to '.avocado/provision-{profile}.state' when not specified.

extensionConfig.title: Extension configuration
extensionConfig.description: Configuration for an Avocado OS extension (system extension or configuration extension). Extensions can be local (defined in this file) or remote (fetched from a source).

source.description: Source configuration for remote extensions. If not specified, the extension is considered local and must be fully defined in this config.

types.title: Extension types
types.description: Extension types. 'sysext' for system extensions that provide system-level functionality, 'confext' for configuration extensions that provide configuration files.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Extension scopes that define where the extension applies. Used to control which parts of the system the extension affects.

overlay.title: Overlay directory
overlay.description: Path to the overlay directory containing files to be included in the extension. The value should be relative to the src_dir. These files will be overlaid onto the root filesystem.

enable_services.title: Enable services
enable_services.description: List of systemd services to enable when the extension is active. Services must be available in the extension or base system.

modprobe.title: Kernel modules
modprobe.description: Kernel modules to load when the extension is active. Modules must be available in the kernel or provided by the extension.

packages.description: Package dependencies required by the extension. These will be installed when the extension is built or deployed.

sdk.description: SDK-specific configuration for building this extension.

packages.description: Build-time packages specific to this extension.

users.description: User accounts to create or configure when the extension is active.

groups.description: Groups to create or configure when the extension is active.

filesystem.title: Filesystem format
filesystem.description: Filesystem format for the extension's .raw image. 'squashfs' (default) is widely supported. 'erofs' offers better random-access read performance and smaller images for some workloads.

var_files.title: Var files
var_files.description: Glob patterns for files within the extension sysroot that belong on the var partition rather than the read-only .raw image. Files matching these patterns are excluded from the image during 'avocado ext image'. Paths are relative to the sysroot root.

docker_images.title: Docker images
docker_images.description: Docker images to pull and pre-cache onto the var partition at build time. When declared, the CLI automatically adds --privileged to the SDK container args and runs a temporary dockerd to pull images for the target architecture. Requires var_files to exclude var/lib/docker/** from the .raw image.

dockerImageRef.title: Docker image reference
dockerImageRef.description: A Docker image reference for var partition priming.

image.description: Full image reference including registry and repository (e.g., docker.io/library/redis).

password.description: User password. Use empty string for no password (passwordless). For security, consider using password hashes instead of plain text.

shell.description: User's login shell. Must be a valid shell available in the system.

home.description: User's home directory path. Will be created if it doesn't exist.

groups.description: List of groups the user belongs to. Groups must exist or be defined in the extension.

gid.description: Group ID (GID). Should be unique across the system. Consider using IDs above 1000 for user groups.

packages.title: Packages
packages.description: Object defining package dependencies with version constraints.

^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$.description: Package name with version constraint. Can be a simple version string or an object with additional configuration.

compile.description: Reference to a compile configuration section in sdk.compile.

install.description: Path to an install script for this package.
